hypothesis: E630/E643/E644's shared open item ('smoke_hero_01's regression remains untraced') has never been independently investigated. E630's own gate docstring explicitly left 'variadic' (array-item) frames permissive, reasoning that the traced over-stuffing failure mode 'only ever hit component/object argument positions, not array items' -- an assumption, not itself traced. Given E628's frame_depth==0 precedent (a bare slot token is grammar-legal at the same decode position as opening a real component, so any margin>0 wins once schema-eligible), the same class of vulnerability plausibly exists at variadic array-item positions too, which E630's fix did not cover. This session traces smoke_hero_01's actual decode on a freshly-trained, same-recipe scratch checkpoint (the original E626/E639 checkpoint no longer exists on disk in this environment -- outputs/ is ephemeral across sessions) to test that hypothesis directly.
environment_note: The exact checkpoint E630/E643/E644 traced (outputs/runs/e626-required-slot-margin-scratch800-20260720/checkpoints/last.pt, sha256 c5b7c807...dd561221) is not present in this session's filesystem (outputs/ is untracked/ephemeral, not committed). A fresh scratch checkpoint was trained using E626/E620's exact documented recipe and seed; its last_loss (4.062225) is close to but not identical to E626/E639's (4.068013), consistent with environment/torch-version nondeterminism across sessions rather than a faithful bit-identical replay. This is disclosed explicitly: results below are on a new, non-identical (but same-recipe) checkpoint, not a verbatim reuse.

part_1_baseline_regression_check.method: Before any code change, replayed a margin sweep {0,1,2,3,4,6} on this fresh checkpoint against src/slm_training/resources/data/eval/remediated's smoke suite (n=3) with E630's exact matched flags (honest_slot_contract, slot_contract_constrained_decode, slot_contract_in_context, semantic_role_contract_in_context, the full semantic_plan_* family, schema_*_decode_weight family, grammar_ltr_max_tokens=160).
part_1_baseline_regression_check.result: smoke_hero_01's meaningful_program_v1 stayed True at every margin in {0,1,2,3,4,6} on this checkpoint -- the exact True->False (empty_root_stack) flip E630/E643/E644 reported on their checkpoint did NOT reproduce here.
part_1_baseline_regression_check.honest_reading: This is expected once the checkpoint itself differs (different init/optimizer trajectory under a different torch build, per environment_note above) -- greedy decode is path-sensitive and this lineage's own E630/E643 findings already established that several of these 19 records are dominated by which-bias-fires-first fragility on an undertrained 800-step scratch checkpoint. A non-reproduction on a different checkpoint does not itself refute the original report; it means this session cannot lean on 'the record now fails' as its evidence and instead had to inspect the decode mechanism directly.

part_2_trace_finds_the_mechanism_anyway.method: Read decode_stats.constrained_selection_traces (phase=='required_slot_margin', E627/E640 instrumentation, already surfaced end to end through scripts.evaluate_model's per-suite eval JSON -- no new plumbing needed) for smoke_hero_01 specifically at margin=2.
part_2_trace_finds_the_mechanism_anyway.finding: 7 required_slot_margin fires on smoke_hero_01; 1 of 7 is hijacked_non_slot_candidate=true at position 14, frame_depth=1, frame_path=[{kind: variadic, expr_type: array, arg_index: 0}] (aggregation_scope: structural_root_list -- literally root's own Stack([...]) children list). The pre-bias argmax was +TextContent (score_before=16.148, a real component-opening candidate that would have been chosen), but the bias's floor (old_max + margin - target_score = 5.661) pushed a slot-fill token (@2, score_after=18.148) above it, replacing a real component with a bare slot reference at the array-item position.
part_2_trace_finds_the_mechanism_anyway.mechanism: Identical in kind to E630's traced component/object over-stuffing mechanism and E628's frame_depth==0 root hijack: the grammar legally allows a bare visible-slot token as an array item at the exact same decode position as opening a real component (frame.kind=='variadic', no item schema -- an untyped/heterogeneous array, e.g. Stack's own children), so _required_slot_margin_bias's floor (constructed to always exceed the current max) wins there too, at any margin>0. E630's own schema-position gate explicitly did not cover this: its docstring stated variadic frames are 'left permissive... since this bias's only observed failure mode is stuffing missing slots into optional enum/opaque component/object properties, not array items' -- an untested assumption this trace falsifies directly.
part_2_trace_finds_the_mechanism_anyway.caveat: This is a mechanism match, not a proven identity with E630/E643/E644's original smoke_hero_01 failure (that checkpoint is unavailable to re-trace). It is offered as the most concrete, evidence-grounded explanation produced for this open item to date, not a certainty.

part_3_fix.change: Extended the E630 schema-position gate to 'variadic' frames: uses the array's own item schema (frame.schemas[0], the tokenizer's own ChoiceDecodeState._active_schema convention for variadic frames) the same way the 'object' frame case already used TwoTowerModel._schema_can_reach_visible_slot. An untyped/heterogeneous array (schemas empty -- meaning any expression, including a real component, is grammar-legal there) or a typed item schema that cannot reach a visible slot now gates the bias closed; a genuinely slot-reachable typed-array item schema still fires unchanged. 'component' and 'object' gating (E630) and 'fixed'-frame permissiveness are unchanged.
part_3_fix.default_and_gating_unchanged: required_slot_margin_decode_weight stays default 0.0 (off); the E617 slot_contract_constrained_decode contract-gated ValueError guard is untouched.

unit_tests.updated: tests/test_models/test_compiler_decode.py::test_required_slot_margin_bias_excludes_non_content_schema_positions (the pre-existing variadic case, which asserted the old permissive behavior with an empty schemas tuple, now asserts the gated no-fire; a comment points at the new dedicated test)
unit_tests.new: tests/test_models/test_compiler_decode.py::test_required_slot_margin_bias_excludes_variadic_positions (no item schema -> no fire; item schema that cannot reach a visible slot -> no fire; item schema that can reach a visible slot -> fires unchanged)

part_3_fix.test_results: 147/147 passed across tests/test_models/test_compiler_decode.py, test_choice_tokenizer.py, test_decode_stats.py (PYTHONPATH=src NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" python -m pytest). python -m scripts.verify_version_stamps --check: ok after the versions.json bump.

part_4_reverification.suite: union of held_out(5)+rico_held(3)+adversarial(4)+ood(4)+smoke(3), n=19 (same union E642/E643/E644 used), still below DEFAULT_MIN_SUITE_N=20 per suite -- exploratory, not a ship scoreboard

smoke_hero_01_across_margins.note: unchanged True at every margin -- the position-14 +TextContent hijack traced in part_2 is confirmed gone from smoke_hero_01's own required_slot_margin trace at margin=2 (only slot-vs-slot swaps and one slot-vs-bind-reference hijack remain, see below).

pooled_meaningful_program_v1_rate.note: 15/19 -> 16/19, a net gain, not flat or negative like every prior sweep in this lineage (E629/E630/E631 all found flat-or-negative pooled effects after closing an over-stuffing gap). Exactly one record differs from control at margin>=1: held_out_dual_card_01 (False -> True), unrelated to the traced variadic-frame fix itself -- margin0's own prediction for that record already fails E644's empty_children:Carousel check on a 44-item degenerate Card; margin>=1 produces a clean 5-item Card instead.

rico_eval_test_25.note: stays fixed (E643), unaffected by this session's change

ood_dashboard_01.note: E643's revert of E639/E641's gain is unaffected -- byte-identical across margins, as it already was pre-fix

residual_hijacks_post_fix.note: A different, narrower class of hijacked_non_slot_candidate=true fires still occur post-fix, all at variadic-frame positions whose item schema IS slot-reachable (so the new gate correctly leaves them active) but where the pre-bias argmax happened to be a bind-reference token (kind='bind', e.g. '&2') rather than a component-opening token. Counts across the n=19 union at margin=2: held_out 1/14, rico_held 4/16, smoke 1/7, adversarial 0/6, ood 0/12. This is a materially different (and so far non-catastrophic on this checkpoint) failure class -- redirecting a reference instead of stealing a real component -- not fixed or scoped by this session's change; flagged as the next open item.

verdict: The specific True->False empty_root_stack flip E630/E643/E644 reported for smoke_hero_01 did not reproduce on this session's freshly-trained (same-recipe, non-identical) checkpoint -- so this is not a confirmed root-cause-and-fix of that exact prior observation. What this session does establish with direct trace evidence: E630's schema-position gate left an identical-in-kind vulnerability open at 'variadic' array-item positions (untested, admitted as an assumption in its own docstring), it fires and hijacks a real component candidate on smoke_hero_01 itself on this checkpoint, and closing it (mirroring E630's own component/object gate, extended via the array's item schema) is a net positive on the widened n=19 suite (15/19 -> 16/19, plus consistent gains on reward/fidelity/structural-similarity, no regressions on any previously-fixed record) -- the first net-positive result in this lineage's own repeated re-verification pattern (E629/E630/E631 all found flat-or-negative pooled effects). Single scratch checkpoint (800 steps, seed 0), n=19 below DEFAULT_MIN_SUITE_N=20 per suite; not a ship claim; no checkpoint trained, promoted, or synced; default required_slot_margin_decode_weight (0.0) unchanged.
next_step: 1) A residual, narrower hijack class (variadic-frame slot-vs-bind-reference, not slot-vs-component) still fires post-fix and was not root-caused here -- worth its own trace if this lever is pursued further. 2) E620's original coverage-aware component/property closure recommendation remains the next lever this lineage has not directly tried. 3) A genuinely powered multi-seed/retrained comparison of required_slot_margin_decode_weight remains open (E626's own deferral, still not picked up). 4) If this environment persists checkpoints across sessions in the future, a direct re-trace of E630/E643/E644's exact original checkpoint would let this finding be confirmed (not just mechanism-matched) against the original report.
commands_run: npm ci (repo root, for AgentV SDK) && npm ci (src/apps/openui_bridge, for the DSL parser bridge used by some tests)

python -m scripts.train_model --train-dir src/slm_training/resources/data/train/e530_visible_semantic_roles_r2_20260719 --model twotower --context-backend scratch --output-tokenizer choice --seed 0 --batch-size 1 --steps 800 --run-id e645-smoke-hero-trace-scratch800-20260721 --run-root outputs/runs --no-sync-checkpoints
python -m scripts.evaluate_model ... --suite smoke --required-slot-margin-decode-weight {0,1,2,3,4,6} (pre-fix baseline check + trace read)
